12152124242511.content:
Both Pokémon stared at Joshua with a strange and suspicious look at first, snarling at him. But then the wondrous scent of the PokéSnax hit their nostrils causing both of the conscious Pokémon to salivate and lick their lips. Houndour looked over to Krokorok.

“What should we do? Do we really give up this girl for those snacks? It took you a while to knock her out right?”

The Krokorok scratched the back of his head.

“Nngh...It did take a bit of work to knock this puppy out. It wasn’t too rough though, was just annoying getting her to stop moving out of the way.”

“Maybe we can just beat this human and take the snacks from him?”

Krokorok stared at the PokéSnax in your hands and stared back at the feet of the Growlithe’s feet he was just worshiping.

“That sounds like a recipe to ruin the snacks. I say we take the deal. We can find another Growlithe to battle and worship almost anytime but these snacks might be a once in a life time experience!”

Houndour nodded.

“Yep, sounds good to me. I was about done with this weakling anyway.”

“Yeah, I’m satisfied myself. These snacks will make a good treat to end things on.”

Both Pokémon then walked up to you, the Krokorok holding his hand out greedily.

“You’ve got a deal human. Hand over those snacks and you can do whatever you please with this Growlithe.”

Joshua smiled, happily dropping the snacks into the Krokorok’s hands.

(-2 Poké Snax / 3 Left)

The Krokorok gave a large a toothy grin, throwing one of the treats towards Houndour who caught it in her mouth.

“Heh, nice trading with you. Now let’s get out of this guy’s way, Houndour.”

Houndour nodded as she munched on the treat, the two pair quickly turning around and walking away from Joshua while they chatted about the next Pokémon they should battle.”

Now Joshua was all alone with the unconscious Growlithe, who was still laying on the ground. Even after all that worshiping had made her soles slick with saliva, it was still clear they were extremely grimy. Her chest rose and fell as she slumbered, her large toes wiggling and splaying every so often. Joshua thought he should probably try and rouse her back to consciousness, but another thought started to come to mind as well...

1215212424251122.content:
Now being so up close to the Growlithe, Joshua could see that the Pokémon’s body was covered in a lot of dirt and gravel. No doubt that the Krokorok had probably used one or two ground attacks in the battle to subdue her. The Growlithe’s tongue was sticking out of her mouth, her face resting in a puddle of her own drool.

But Joshua didn’t really care about that, he was much more interested in the feet right in front of him. Perhaps unsurprisingly, the Growlithe’s feet were quite hot, with there even being a warm musk wafting from the unconscious Pokémon’s feet. Joshua could see that there was still a layer of black grime on the Growlithe’s pudgy soles, with some dirt and gravel having deftly stuck themselves there. Joshua also started to note the heavy amount of sweat that coated the Pokémon’s body which of course also included her feet.

Joshua’s thoughts got the better of him, and he started to suck on the Growlithe’s meaty toes. It didn’t take long at all for the salty and warm taste to explode in his mouth, causing Josh to moan blissfully. There was also the sour aftertaste of the Krokorok’s and Houndour’s own saliva mixed in with the Growlithe’s natural taste which made things even better. The young man also used his tongue to weave in-between the meaty digits, shoveling out the clumps of grime that had been built up there.

After having his fill with the toes, Josh moved on to the Growlithe’s lovely soles. However, he had only managed to get a few licks in before he started to hear some murmuring.

“Nngh....nnnnh...”

Josh quickly dropped the foot in his hand, going to grab his camera and shut it off before the Growlithe fully woke up. Putting the camera away, Joshua managed to put everything away before the Puppy Pokémon woke back up.

“Uuuugh...guuuuh...” After letting out a few more pained moans, the Growlithe’s eyes started to slowly flutter open. “Nnngh...my head...it still hurts...how many times did that Krokorok and Houndour knock me out...” The Growlithe slowly and shakily got back up onto her feet, looking around before noticing Joshua in front of her. “Huh? Who are you? Where did Krokorok and Houndour go...”

“Oh uh...” Joshua sheepishly scratched the back of his head. “I found them uh...worshiping your feet while you were unconscious. So I managed to get them to leave you alone.”

“Oh...” The Growlithe still seemed a bit out of it, but there was definitely a sense of gratefulness behind her weak and shaky soft voice. “Nngh...I guess I should thank you for rescuing me. Who knows how many times those two were going to knock me out before they got bored for the day. It...it starts adding up in the end...”

Josh’s eyes bulged in surprise.

“Wait, those two have been knocking you out all day?”

“Yeah...it’s not an uncommon thing to happen to the weaker Pokémon on the island. One or two Pokémon will make them their target for the day, let them try and get away or fight back for a bit before they knock them out. Then they worship their feet and after they wake back up, they do it all over again...”

“I...I see.” Joshua was surprised to hear about such behavior. While Pokémon did fight in the wild, what the Growlithe was describing here didn’t sound like anything Joshua has ever heard of. The idea of a Pokémon constantly knocking out another all day, just for the thrill of both the hunt and the prize of their feet just seemed so out there. Thinking about it, was the hunt part specifically to get the victim’s feet dirty again after a worship session? Perhaps that’s why the Growlithe’s feet were so absolutely filthy, she had been being chased around all day for the brief times she’s been awake. “...I’m sorry to hear that...”

The Growlithe let out a exasperated sigh, her pudgy toes wiggling a bit.

“No, there’s nothing to be sorry about. The weak only exist to be used by the strong here. It’s my fault for being so weak. If only I could get stronger...” The Growlithe looked down to the ground and then looked back up at you, a bit of a glint in her eyes. “Actually, maybe I could get stronger if I stuck with you? I’ve heard about how some Pokémon here ended up allying themselves with you humans and have gotten stronger for it. So uh...if it doesn’t bother you, I’d like to stick with you at least as thanks for you saving me from a few more hours of being hunted.”

The Growlithe nervously rubbed her foot against the hot ground, looking at Joshua expectedly.

While Pokémon outside the main land were expressly forbidden from coming to Footprint Island, Ally had manage to convince those in charge to at least let her bring Kat with the explicit promise that he wouldn’t disturb the ecosystem of the island.

Starting their journey proper, Ally and Kat were dropped off near the Crater of the Volcano. Smoke was bellowing out of the open hole, but more importantly it was absolutely nearly deathly hot up here. Ally and Kat started sweating immediately, Ally’s skin and clothes and Kat’s fur respective becoming drenched in sweat as the heat hit every part of their body. Ally could feel the hot rocky ground through her shoes and socks, swearing every time she looked down that she’d find them burned away and see her own feet and black-painted toenails. Kat who was barefoot meanwhile, seemed to be able to take the ground pretty well as some hot gravel and dirt got stuck to their soles.

Luckily, it didn’t take too long for the pair to find their first Pokémon. After walking around for a bit, they came across...

“...try and talk with it? Maybe it’s friendly?”

The Marowak looked down at Ally and Kat’s feet. It seemed to scoff at Ally’s shoed feet, but looking at Kat’s big feet made the Marowak lick her lips and wiggle her own toes in excitement.

“Mmm...now those are some real good looking feet. Maybe that human even has some good ones underneath those horrible things they trap them in.” The Marowak started to dance, swinging it’s flaming bone like it was a baton. They kicked up dirt and dust with each step and after finishing the tribal dance, they got into a fighting stance. “Now then, let’s fight! I’ll enjoy worshiping those feet after I knock both of you out!”
131112.title: Looking for a fight!

choices: Deal!
No, but if you beat me I suppose I can lend a hand...
Not a chance! (Attacks)
The Marowak agrees, but quickly gets second thoughts
Third Party! (Extra pokemon from out of no where!)
1311121.content:
"Kat, are you up for the fight?" Ally asks her faithful Kat, wiping some sweat from her forehead. Kat nods back.
"You saw it, he did his tribal dance, it would be rude of me to deny him a battle" Kat replies, then turns to the Marowak, getting into her stance. "Besides, it's been a while since I got to fight too!"

Ally smiles. "Then, a battle it is! I'll get your back Kat!" She says getting ready too. "And how about a wager? Looser becomes the servant of the winner" She says to the Marowak.
1311121.title: Accept the Marowak’s challenge and Fight

choices: Kat is hit by the attack and is sent flying!
Kat is hit by the attack but she manages to get in an attack of her own!
Kat dodges the kick and counter attacks!
13111211.content:
The Marowak gave a confident snort, gripping it’s bone tightly as it grinded it’s sole against the hot ground.

“Heh, of course I’ll take that deal! It’ll be too easy to knock you two out so it just sounds like you’re happily giving yourselves up to me! I already can think of a few things I’ll do with the both of you after you’re my servants! Now let’s do this!”

The Alolan Marowak quickly took the offensive, leaping towards Kat as she surrounded her right foot with fire. Letting out a shrill battle cry, the Marowak aimed to hit Kat in the gut with her flaming and dirty foot!

"...Aura Sphere!"
"...Grind your foot in her face!"

Her attack was successful, getting a nice hit on Kat who attempted to block it, managing to get some means of defense but still taking the brunt of the flaming kick, leaving a lightly singed print on her gut. However, she also had grabbed ahold of the Marowak's ankle, and quickly swing around and tossed her off to a nearby boulder! Though weakened lightly from the burning sensation of the kick, she puts a fair amount of strength into, and the impact leaves a nice couple cracks in the rock.

"You get em Kat!" Ally cheers on. "Now, get em with..."

1311121121.content:
“...with a kick of your own!”

Kat nodded, the Riolu dashing towards the Alolan Marowak who was just getting back onto her feet from the Riolu’s throw. The Emanation Pokémon gritted his teeth, trying to decide which part of the body to hit. Quickly, Kat decided to deliver a roundhouse kick to the Marowak’s side.

As the distance between the two Pokémon began to close...
1311121121.title: "...A follow up kick!"

choices: Yes... You beat me (Win)
No, not yet...! (Suprise attack!)
Wound up knocked-out from the kick (Win)
13111211213.content:
As Kat closed the distance, Marowak gothers herself enough to start flicking her bone, spitting fireballs out at Kat!
Quickly she dodges them, strafing left or right, then jumping over the third, changing her kick target from the side to the face of Aloan pokemon, giving her a face full of dirty sweaty 'Cario sole, even leaving behind some of the grind as a paw-print right where the kick landed, along with a red mark.
Afterword's she does a hop backwards, flipping off the face of the Marowak. Huffing some; "H-Huh... H-had enough?"
She says confidently.
13111211213.title: Marowak flings a fireball from her bone!

choices: Kat is knocked out by the powerful Bonemerang! (Lose)
Kat takes the attack well and goes to knock the Marowak out! (Win)
Kat is knocked out but Marowak faints from her injuries too (Double KO!)
131112112132.content:
Such a strong kick to the face would have knocked out any other Pokémon. But the hardened skull on the Marowak’s face allowed it to lessen the damage she would have taken, and even with that it was clear she was still quite dazed from the attack! The Marowak stumbled around for a bit, Kat’s dirty pawprint prominently showing on her face like some kind of marking.

“Tch, I must admit that you’re much stronger than I imagined. But you’re not strong enough!” The Marowak started to do another tribal dance, as a sickly green flame started to swirl around it’s bone. After a spin, the flames quickly snaked over to Kat, trapping the Riolu in a twister of green flame!

“Nngh...what is this?” Just being in the twister seemed to weaken Kat, the Riolu falling onto their knees as their pudgy and dirty toes wiggled. Kat shakily got back onto her feet, resolving to get out of the fiery tornado. But before the Riolu could even attempt to power through the flames, the Marowak’s bone came spinning towards Kat hitting the Riolu right in the gut.

“Guuuuugh!!!” The Riolu let out a gasp, as some spittle flew out of Kat’s mouth. A red bruise appeared on her stomach as she fell onto one knee. She held her bruised gut as the flames died down and the Marowak became visible again right as she caught her bone, the Marowak also being extremely tired as sweat ran down her body. The Marowak also feel to one knee, the kick probably doing even more damage than she initially thought, her head spinning around as a bit of drool started to run out of her mouth.

“Nngh...had enough little Riolu?”
131112112132.title: No, not yet...! (Suprise attack!)

132.content:
Name: Eleanora

Gender: Female

Appearance: Eleanora is a tall young woman with an extremely pale complexion and crimson red eyes. She has long and straight brown hair and a bridge of freckles across her nose. She wears a a silky light blue dress that flows down to her ankles and white evening gloves as her only article of clothing. She also has a pair of blue heeled sandals, but she takes them off while exploring the island, believing that going barefoot will make the Pokémon less wary of her. She has sparkly silver nail polish on her toenails.

choices: A Machop (Male) with five-toes training amongst himself.
A Flareon (Female) happily sunbathing on a heated rock!
A Salazzle using her pheromones to control a Charmeleon (Male)!
1321.content:
Eleanora soon found herself dropped off near the smoking crater of the Volcano. Almost immediately, the young woman felt the blistering heat of the volcano causing her to sweat heavily and soak her blue dress. The hot ground heated Eleanora’s feet through her heels as well.

Luckily for her, it didn’t take too long for the noble woman to find her first subject which ended up being...
1321.title: Blazing Beginning!

choices: Eleanora decides to try and talk with the Machop
Another Pokemon comes to fight the Machop
13211.content:
As soon as Eleanora was on solid ground again, the first thing she did was take her heels off despite the hostile environment. Considering how foot-centric this entire island seemed to be, it would be strange for her to wear her shoes around here anyway. And despite the heat, the ground didn't seem too bad. In fact, it was decently comfortable to feel that warmth underneath her soles.

Placing her heels into her bag, it didn't take too long for Eleanora to find her first Pokemon and what a sight he was. It was a Machop who was heavily training by himself, destroying a few boulders with his powerful punches and kicks. Sweat glistened gray skin, making it shine in the sun.

Most importantly of course to Eleanora was the Machop's feet. Unlike any other Machop she's ever seen, this Machop had five large and human-like toes, which wiggled and curled with each kick. Eleanora recorded the training session, focusing on the Machop's sweaty feet and soles which were coated in a heavy layer of wet grime. Eleaniora licked her lips, wanting to get a lot closer to those feet than just hiding behind a rock and recording them from afar...
13211.title: A Machop (Male) with five-toes training amongst...

choices: Simply watch and record the entire fight!
Interfere with the fight directly.
Use a Poké Tool!
132112.content:
While you were busy recording however, another Pokémon walked up towards the Machop clearly reading itself for a fight. It was a male Makuhita, with Eleanora noting how it had three pudgy toes instead of the usual two. Like the Machop, the Makuhita was glistening with a copious amount of sweat and it’s feet were extremely dirty.

“Hey, Machop! You ready for me to take you down?”

The Machop swiveled around, getting into a fighting stance.

“Heh, I was wondering if you were ever going to show up. Soon enough, your feet are going to be all mine!”

“You’re wrong about that! The only Pokémon who’s getting knocked out here is you! And I’ll be the one having fun with your feet!”

The two Fighting-Types quickly launched into combat, trading blows with each other of equal strength. Meanwhile, Eleanora was in awe, surprised to hear that the Pokémon here seemed so intent on playing with the other’s feet. She wondered if it was something inherit to all the Pokémon of the island or just these two, as she recorded the fight and made sure to get good captures of both of the Pokémon’s feet in action!
132112.title: Another Pokemon comes to fight the Machop

choices: The Machop knocks out the Makuhita and comes out on top
The Makuhita knocks out the Machop and comes out on top
1321121.content:
Eleanora thought about perhaps interfering in the battle, but she stopped herself not wanting to waste any of her tools so early on her trip. And plus, getting a candid video of two Pokemon fighting on Footprint Island seemed like something that would be good research material for the professor...and a good video for her to watch in her own time.

She kept on recording the battle, the Machop and Makuhita still seeming quite evenly matched. The Machop's battle style was a lot more offensive, using a variety of powerful punches and kicks as he jumped across the battlefield. Eleanora got a good few shots of the Machop's feet and soles as he moved across the battlefield, the Machop kicking dust and gravel into the air with each hop and strife.

Meanwhile, the Makuhita's battle style was a lot more defensive, the Guts Pokemon chuckling to himself as he happily seemed to absorb the Machop's mighty blows. Instead, he focused on countering the Machop's blows with his own, blocking a kick with one arm and then punching the Machop with the other. Alongside these block and punches, he would also sometimes manage to grab the Machop, throwing him onto the ground or into a nearby rock. Unfortunately, the Makuhita's battle style didn't give too many chances to really view the Makuhita's soles, though she did manage to get some good shots of it and the Makuhita's toes wiggling when the Pokemon would stand on his toes to punch up or when a single foot would lift into the air when he would perform one of his throws.

After about 5 minutes of recording, both of the sweaty Fighting Types were at their limit. They panted heavily, large droplets of sweat falling off of their body and evaporating on the hot ground. Their wet and dirty soles seemed to sink into the gravel as they stared down at each other.

"Whahaha! You're...you're much stronger than I imagined...Machop...I thought this would be...much easier..."

"Tch...And I thought the same...seems like that...defensive style is quite powerful...but I'm going to end this battle with the next blow!"

"Heh...took...the words out of...my mouth! Let's finish this!"
1321121.title: Simply watch and record the entire fight!

choices: Eleanora continues recording as the Machop worships the Makuhita’s feet!
Eleanora decides to try and talk with the Machop!
Eleanora decides to use a Poké Tool on the Machop!
13211211.content:
The Machop and Makuhita stared each other down for a bit longer, the intensity in each one’s eyes.

Eventually, the Machop leapt forward, reading his final attack. Upon landing, the Machop dug his heel into the ground to stop himself as he prepared to deliver a powerful roundhouse kick to Makuhita.

The Makuhita grinned bringing up his arms to block the hit, believing he’s won the battle.

However, the Machop had a trick up his sleeve that even the Makuhita couldn’t expect. The Machop waved his arm, sending out a Vacuum Wave at the Makuhita. The attack made it’s mark, hitting the Makuhita and pushing him back. His soles slid across the hot ground and his guard was broken.

“Nngh! What the-“

Before the Makuhita had time to recompose himself, the Machop used the opportunity to deliver a powerful Mega Kick right into the Makuhita’s face and sending the pudgy Pokémon flying.

“Guuuuh..uuunh...” The Makuhita was unconscious before he even hit the ground, his tongue lolling out of his mouth as Eleanora was able to get a really good view of the Makuhita’s dirty soles soaring in the air. Eventually, his fat body fell onto the ground with a loud thud. His pudgy toes wiggled and splayed as his large body twitched around a bit.

The Machop let out a relieved sigh, wiping some sweat from his brow as he took a few deep breaths.

“That battle was even harder then I could I ever imagined. But now those feet are all mine...”

Eleanora could scarcely believe what happened next, even as she was recording it all. The Machop went over and bent down behind the Makuhita’s feet and started sucking on the Makuhita’s toes, clearly aroused with the sweaty and dirty meaty digits in his mouth. Eleanora made sure to get a close-up also watching the Machop’s own toes wiggle in delight with the sucking.
13211211.title: Machop takes the victory!

choices: The Machop is surprisingly docile and listens to Eleanora
The Machop is aggressive towards Eleanora
132112112.content:
Eleanora watched the entire worship session, recording in awe as the Machop started dragging his tongue across the knocked out Makuhita’s grime-covered soles, the Machop clearly aroused by the worship session. Eleanora herself was also quite aroused, her own toes flexing as she imagined herself licking both the Machop’s and Makuhita’s feet. She also made sure to get some good shots of the Machop’s grime-caked soles and his toes wiggling in delight.

It almost made her want to try and knock the Machop unconscious and worship his soles. But she also thought about potentially revealing herself and talking to the Machop. He could potentially give her some nice information about the island and maybe she’d even get something more out of it.

Putting her expensive camcorder away, Eleanora walked over to the Machop with a lady-like grace, her soles picking up hot dirt and gravel. Soon enough, she found herself in front of the Machop.

“Uh, hello. I saw your fight against the Makuhita and was really impressed with how strong you were! Um...if you don’t mind could I ask you some things about the island?”
132112112.title: Eleanora decides to try and talk with the Machop!

The Machop will tell you, if you worship his feet!
The Machop refuses to tell you anything.

As you walked up to the Machop, he let out a tired sigh when he saw you come up though you did see him take a glance at your feet.

"Nngh...what do you want? Can't you see I'm a bit busy here..."

After hearing your request, the Machop closed his eyes thinking about your request. Eventually the Machop decided...

info.brief_description: Head off to Footprint Island, taking pictures of the feet of unconscious Pokemon!
info.description:
Summary


Footprint Island, a newly discovered Island in the Pokémon world that's completely isolated from most other locations. A landmass in the shape of a giant footprint, the little research done there so far has revealed that many of the Pokémon living on the biome rich island have a strange quirk shared among all of them. All of the Pokémon's feet have three to five toes on their feet and even Pokémon that usually don't have feet in other locations seem to have feet on Footprint Island. Thus, the island has become a somewhat hot yet quiet topic of interest in the Pokémon World.

And that's where you come in! Many individuals interested in the Island for a variety of reasons have started to hire Photographers and Videographers to take pictures and film the Pokémon of the Island in their natural habitat. Of course, the Pokémon won't just let any old person walk up to them and start filming or snapping photos of them. The Pokémon of Footprint are both extremely cautious and extremely domineering and brutal, quickly attacking anyone they view as a threat.

As those who come to the Island are forbidden from bringing their own Pokémon, due to fear of crossbreeding and possibly disrupting the island's natural environment, an alternate method for people to protect themselves was devised. These was the c, which allows those on the Island to deal with the Pokémon in a variety of ways in order to get those shots of their feet.

You are just one of the many people who have come to this Island, whether it be for actual research, for more ulterior motives or to fulfill your own wicked fantasies. Now it's time for you to start your journey on Footprint Island and get those shots of Pokémon feet!

Rules / Gameplay


This interactive story is quite highly inspired by the stories such as the Smelly Pokémon Series, thus it's a bit more game-like than the usual interactive.

In this interactive, you play the role of someone who has come to the island to document the Pokémon that live there for whatever reason. Meanwhile, another person (This may end up being me a good chunk of the time, but other users can definitely take up this role and are encouraged to do so!) essentially play out the Pokémon that you meet and the events that you run into. Your main goal is to take as many photographs of the feet of Pokémon as you possibly can until you either get a game over or your character simply leaves the island. While you may get lucky and find a good amount of Pokémon just sleeping, or one that had been knocked out in a recent fight that you can take photos of their feet up close for points, you'll eventually have to knock out a Pokémon or wait for them to be knocked out by other forces to get those really candid photo! Or will you end up coming back empty-handed or even worse, become the foot slave of one of the Pokémon on the island!

Anyway here are the rules for this Interactive!

What is heavily encouraged:

Foot Fetishim +Somnophilia

What's fine:

Stench / Smell Stuff

Hypnotism

Macro / Micro Stuff

Minor Muscle Growth

Dirty Feet (Sweat/ Dirt/ Grime etc.)

Transformation

What's not fine:

Watersport

Diaper

Inflation

Scat

Vore

Death

Overly excessive and extremely gory violence.

Hate Speech or anything related to that.

Other Rules:

While I'm fine with shorter responses, especially knowing how hard it can be to make really long responses they should at least have effort put into them. Low effort responses will be removed.

Remember to stay on the topic of the interactive, while I know most people aren’t super into Somnophilia stuff it’s the main focus of this story alongside the foot fetishim. So most Pokémon you encounter and wish to take a photo of will have to be KO'd instead of just convincing them in other ways normally. However, there are some Poké Tools that can allow you to get photos or worship a Pokémon's feet while they're still conscious, a Pokémon can wake up and you can keep worshiping or playing with their feet depending on certain factors.

Locations


The first thing one does when they come to Footprint Island is decide which area they wish to be dropped off at. There are Eight areas available for drop off and ten in total.

Starting Locations

1: Lush Forest: A somewhat standard looking Forest full of lush greenery. Many different kind of Pokémon make their home here, but it is mostly comprised of Normal, Bug and Grass Types. The forest also has many rivers flowing through it where some water Pokémon may be found. From Lush Jungle, one can either go to Damp Cave or Radiant Beach.

2: Damp Cave: A large and winding cave, which gets wetter and wetter the deeper down you go. Many Rock, Ground and Steel Type Pokémon dwell on the upper floors, while Water, Electric and Dark Type Pokémon dwell on the lower levels. There's an entrance to the cave in Lush Jungle and heading through the tunnel will eventually lead you to Sweltering Volcano.

3: Sweltering Volcano: A scorching non-active volcano where many Fire Types make their home. Rock, Ground, Steel and Fighting Pokémon can also be seen here. From here, one can head into Damp Cave or go towards Arid Valley.

4: Arid Valley: A dry and desolate valley that only the hardest of Pokémon decide to make their home. Poison, Dragon, Fighting and Flying Types are most common Types one will usually find here. One can either head to the Sweltering Volcano or Windy Mountain.

5: Windy Mountain: One of the twin mountains of Footprint Island, it's the home to many of the Island's Flying Pokémon. Some Dragon, Fighting and Rock types also loiter around the area. This area is connected to Arid Valley and Raindrop Jungle.

6: Snow Mountain: The other twin mountain of Footprint Island, it's the near exclusive home of the Island's Ice Type Pokémon. Fighting and Steel Pokémon also make their home here, especially in the tunnels dotted around the mountain. The only location it's connected with is Raindrop Jungle.

7: Raindrop Jungle: A beautiful and large jungle that commonly suffers from rain. It can also be quite hot within the jungle. Grass, Bug, Fire and Water Type Pokémon are abundant in this location with Psychic, Dark and Fairy Pokémon also being common sights. The jungle is connected to Snow Mountain and Radiant Beach.

8: Radiant Beach: A beautiful and sparkling beach, beaten down by the sun and with clear blue water. Water Pokémon are extremely common unsurprisingly but many Normal, Psychic and Fairy Pokémon also make this place their home.

Other Locations:

9: Ghastly Mansion: A mysterious and abandoned looking Mansion that sometimes appears in other areas at random. It is of course the nearly exclusive home of all Ghost Type Pokémon, but some Dark, Psychic and Poison Pokémon can be seen lurking about.

10: Rainbow Sanctuary: A strange area which is rumored to only appear to those who manage to stay on the Island for a long time. Appearing as a large Castle, a large variety of Pokémon can usually be found within it. In the throne room, lies an incredibly strong Legendary Pokémon.

(Of course, most of these are just guidelines! If you think a Pokémon would fit in it with any of their areas but they aren't any of the mentioned types don't worry! It'll usually be fine to place them there!)

Character Creation


After choosing your starting location, you can create your character! Here's what you'll need!

Name:

Gender:

Appearance:

Personality:

Poké Tools: 1. 2. 3.

Favored Type: (This will have some sway on interactions with Pokémon)

Preferred Genders: Your Preferred Genders. for any kind of encounters.

Reason for Coming to the Island:

Interest: How interested is your character in feet before finding themselves on the Island.

Rotom Translator: The Player chooses if they wish to play with the Translator On or Off. If turned on, the player will be able to understand what Pokémon on the island are saying.

Poké Tools


The Poké Tools are super important items that one needs while they journey through Footprint Island, being the only way one can protect themselves from the aggressive Pokémon living on the island. One can choose three Poké Tools to take with them on their journey. Some Poké Tools have a limited amount of uses, while others have a charge period until they can be used again. Here are the Poké Tools one can bring with them.

1. Lull Balls: Small rainbow-colored balls that shoot out a sweet smelling smoke from the holes on it when the pin on top is pulled off. The smoke only affects Pokémon, with them quickly succumbing to the smoke and lulling them into unconsciousness. This sleeping gas is extremely potent, even being able to fell the most powerful of Pokémon within seconds after getting a whiff of the gas. Legendaries and Mythicals can also be adversely affected, as while they may not collapse immediately, their movements will become extremely sluggish and they may even eventually pass out after breathing in enough of the gas. It also has an wide radius, being able to knock out every Pokémon within the area and sometimes even nearby areas if one is lucky. However, Lull Balls are extremely expensive and thus it's usually hard to get a hold on them. (2 Lull Balls Per Quest)

2: Stun Needles: Small needles which can be shot out of a tranquilizer gun, if these needles hit their target, the Pokémon is paralyzed from head to toe and are unable to move. Depending on the type of the Pokémon, the needles may knock them out outright or not be as effective or maybe not work at all. (6 Stun Needles Per Quest)

3: Poison Needles: Small needles which can be shot out of a tranquilizer gun, if these needles hit their target, the Pokémon are injected with a non-lethal Poison that knocks them out. Depending on the type of the Pokémon and how strong they are, more than 1 needle may need to be used or they might not even be affected by them at all. (10 Poison Needles Per Quest)

4: Poké Snax: Tasty snacks that are beloved by many Pokémon. Giving a Pokémon one will usually make it more willing to at least listen to you, allowing you to take photos of your feet, perhaps letting you even play with your feet and even in rare cases, joining you. Though more powerful Pokémon, Legendaries and Mythical will tend to refuse such handouts. If you try to give one to a Pokémon while there's another in the same area it will tend to start a fight between the Pokémon or possibly have them attack you. (5 Poké Snax per Quest.)

5: Rage Powder: A powder hidden with a small ball, which is released when the ball is thrown towards the ground and explodes. Once the powder is released, it causes all Pokémon in the area to become more violent and start fighting against each other until there is only one Pokémon left standing. Usually the last Pokémon will faint from exhaustion at that point, though sometimes they may instead turn their ire to you. Of course, this could also happen earlier if a Pokémon happens to find you during the Powder induced riot. Rage Powder cannot affect Legendary and Mythical Pokémon. (3 Rage Powder per Quest.)

6: Super Effective Buster: A strange rod-like item that has a Ditto-like figure attached to the top. When going up against a Pokémon, the ditto figure will change and use an attack that is super-effective against that Pokémon. This attack will almost always knock out the Pokémon it targeted and even Legendary and Mythical Pokémon will at least take a large amount of damage, usually making it much easier to knock them out or do something else. However, the item takes a large time to recharge, with the Ditto figure slowly regrowing with time. (Charge Time: 30 Responses.)

7: Hypno Pendulum: A contraption similar to a Hypno's pendulum, it can unsurprisingly be used to hypnotize a Pokémon you encounter. Some Pokémon can be weaker or more resistant to the hypnosis depending on their typing, and it's also harder to hypnotize more powerful Pokémon unless they are sufficiently weakened. Legendary and Mythical Pokémon usually can't be hypnotized but if they're greatly weakened, it may be possible to hypnotize them. Hypnotized Pokémon will follow your every order and will also become followers, however there's a chance that the hypnotism will break and if that happens, things won't be pretty. One can try and prevent by using the Pendulum on an already hypnotized Pokémon to bring them to a deeper state of hypnosis. (Charge Time: 10 Responses.)

8: Luvdisc Spray: A spray that one can apply to their own body, the scent making it somewhat likely that a Pokémon will be attracted to you and makes it more likely that they'll listen to your demands or even join you. Unlike hypnotism, Pokémon that end up being attracted will not break out or betray you. Legendary and Mythical Pokémon cannot be attracted. (Charge Time: 15 Responses.)

9: Boxploud: A large box in the shape of an Exploud head. When the button is pressed, it will emit a loud screech that will stop almost any Pokémon in their track. While it can cause some extremely weak Pokémon to faint, it's main purpose is to be used to get away from bad institutions that would be impossible to get out of normally. (Charge Time: 6 Responses.)

10: Stealth Coat: A coat based off a Kecleon's skin, one can use it to practically turn invisible to the naked eye. This allows you to sneak up to Pokémon and get some nice candid shots without them suspecting a thing. It wears off after you leave the area you activated it or if you end up hitting something. Also, certain Pokémon will be able to see you even if you're invisible. (Charge Time: 12 Response.)

Pokémon


Of course, your character will encounter Pokémon on the Island and have to interact with them in a variety of way. The Pokémon of Footprint Island come in 7 different Power Levels.

Pathetic: The lowest of the low in terms of Power. You could probably honestly fight against them on your own and come out on top.

Weak: Extremely weak Pokémon, who you can easily beat as long as you're paying attention and keeping one step ahead of them.

Average: The standard and most common Power Level of the Pokémon of the Island, these Pokémon can be somewhat difficult to deal though they still tend to be manageable in most situations.

Strong: Stronger Pokémon roaming around the area which are most harder to deal with one your own. They usually take a lot more punishment and thinking to take down compared to their Average kin.

Vigorous: The strongest level that normal Pokémon of the Island can possibly attain, sometimes even getting close to the same amount of power of a Mythical Pokémon. They're able to shrug off multiple effects and damage and are extremely hard to take down. Vigorous Pokémon are usually seen as leaders of the area they live in and you can garner respect from the other Pokémon if you can defeat one or two.

Mythical: The Power Level given to rare Pokémon who's very existence are questioned. These Pokémon can be extremely hard to find and even if you find them, they can be extremely hard to take down. It is said that Mythicals are much more likely to become your follower after being defeated than other Pokémon, but that means you'd have to beat them first.

Legendary: The highest Power Level, unsurprisingly given to the Legendary Pokémon living in the area. Usually limited to one per area, they can seem impossible to defeat even if one comes fully prepared to take them down. If you beat one however, all the other Pokémon of the area will instantly respect you and acquiesce to most of your demands.

Followers


There are times where you'll manage to get a Pokémon to join your side. Whether it be by pleasuring them, helping them in some sort of situation, on the Pokémon's own seemingly random whims, or even attracting or hypnotizing them, you can potentially end up having a Pokémon as a Follower.

A Follower is a Pokémon that stays by your side, assisting you while traverse the island in various ways by using their moves and abilities. Getting one Follower can already be an extremely difficult task and having one can make gaining another much harder. The maximum amount of Followers one character can have is three.

These are the stats a follower will have.

Name:

Species:

Gender:

Type:

Behavior: Dominant (Tend to use more Offensive Moves and Abilities.) / Submissive (Tend to use more Supportive Moves and Abilities.)

State: Normal (For Followers who willingly join your cause) / Hypnotized (For those hypnotized either through the Hypno Pendulum or through some other means. There are 5 Levels of Hypnosis: Weak (Very weak Hypnosis, extremely easy to break out of at any time.) / Lesser (A lesser state of Hypnosis that's a decent amount stronger than Weak, it's still quite easily broken out of.) / Average (The Standard Level of Hypnosis, which has a moderate chance to be broken) / High (A powerful Level of Hypnosis which can nearly be impossible to break out of for most Pokémon.) / Deep (The deepest level of Hypnosis which cannot be broken outside of some truly extreme circumstances. At this level, the hypnotized Pokémon has basically become completely and utterly subservient toy you.)

And that's about it for the information on this Interactive Story. Doubt it'll be too popular, but I do hope some people with similar interest or even not so similar interest still find this little thing fun and try it out!
